# Arbiter

> Arbiter is a developer-first MCP (Model Context Protocol) gateway for AI agents.

## What it does
- Routes AI agent tool calls through a central proxy with access control
- Manages agent identities via scoped API keys
- Enforces tool-level permissions per agent — control exactly which MCP tools each agent can call
- Stores secrets in an encrypted vault accessible to agents at runtime
- Caches semantically similar tool calls to reduce cost and latency
- Provides full session observability — every tool call, every agent, every session, logged

## Who it's for
Developers building AI agents with Claude, GPT, or any MCP-compatible client who need production-grade access control, secrets management, and observability without building all of that themselves.

## Pricing
Free tier available. No credit card required to start.

## Links
- Dashboard: https://arbiterai.dev
- API: https://nexusai-api-production.up.railway.app
- GitHub: https://github.com/JaidenSy/Arbiter

## MCP Server Authentication

MCP servers registered in Arbiter can require authentication headers (e.g. a GitHub Personal Access Token). Arbiter supports vault-referenced header values so secrets are never stored in plaintext.

### How to authenticate an MCP server

**Step 1 — Store the secret in the Vault**
In the Arbiter dashboard, go to Vault → Add Secret.
- Name: `github_pat` (or any slug you choose)
- Value: your raw secret (e.g. `Bearer ghp_xxxx...`)

The secret is encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.

**Step 2 — Reference it in the MCP server headers**
When adding or editing an MCP server, add an Auth Header:
- Header name: `Authorization`
- Value: `{{vault:github_pat}}`

At proxy time, Arbiter resolves `{{vault:github_pat}}` to the decrypted secret before forwarding the request. The raw token never appears in the database or logs.

**Step 3 — Grant agent permissions and call tools normally**

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.arbiterai.dev/api/v1/proxy/tool-call \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nxai_..." \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"server_name": "github", "tool_name": "search_repositories", "params": {"query": "mcp"}}'
```

### Placeholder syntax

`{{vault:SECRET_NAME}}` can appear anywhere in a header value string, including mid-string:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{vault:github_pat}}
X-Api-Key: {{vault:my_api_key}}
```

Multiple placeholders in one value are supported.
